stem: An alloy consisting of only silver and tin has a total mass of 240 grams. The mass of silver in the alloy is 90 grams. What is the mass, in grams, of tin in the alloy?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The total mass is the sum of silver and tin masses, so 240 = 90 + tin. Subtracting 90 from each side gives tin = 150 grams.

Choice A is the silver mass. Choice C is the total. Choice D incorrectly adds rather than subtracts.
